checks.a_analysis: The output content closely follows the source text. There are no entirely invented paragraphs, section headings, or body text. The repetitive header 'NC DIT's FirstTech Annual Review 2020' on each page of the output matches the header present on each page of the source document.

There are no fabricated phone numbers, emails, or URLs. While the contact information (email and URL) at the end of the document is missing from the output, it has not been replaced with incorrect or hallucinated data.

All pages from the source are represented in the output. All primary headings ('Executive Summary', 'Vision', 'Mission', 'Accomplishments', 'Industry Engagements', 'Partnership Engagements/Economic Development', 'Emergency Response', 'Program Management', 'Future', 'Contact') are present.
The source document does not contain any tables.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: There is a significant loss of information in several list-based sections and the 'Contact' section. Multiple sub-bullets found in the source PDF are entirely missing from the output. Most critically, the contact details (email address and website URL) are missing from the 'Contact' section, leaving only fragments of the surrounding sentence. This represents a loss of over 25% of the content within the 'Partnership Engagements' and 'Contact' sections.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 2: Sub-bullet 'o This led to tremendous collaboration efforts...'; Sub-bullets under 'On-going participation in several efforts with DHS...'; Sub-bullet under 'FirstTech staff presented at several conferences...'. Page 3: 'firsttech@nc.gov and www.ncbroadband.gov/firsttech/'
checks.b_evidence_output: The output list items for these sections omit the sub-bullets. The 'Contact' section (elements 30 and 32) contains 'FirstTech can be reached at ' and ' and ', but the actual contact data is missing.
checks.b_evidence_location: Sections 'Industry Engagements', 'Partnership Engagements/Economic Development', and 'Contact' on pages 2 and 3.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: Aside from the major omissions noted in C5, the text that is present faithfully represents the wording and meaning of the source document. Punctuation and formatting are handled within acceptable limits.

checks.a_analysis: The document title 'NC DIT's FirstTech Annual Review 2020' appears three times in the output (Reading orders 1, 13, and 20), but this corresponds to its appearance as a header on each of the three pages in the source document. No other paragraphs, list items, or sections appear to be duplicated incorrectly.

The heading hierarchy is inconsistent. Under the major section 'Accomplishments' (H2), the first subsection 'Responder Engagements' is assigned H3 (element 10). However, subsequent parallel subsections 'Industry Engagements' (element 15), 'Partnership Engagements/Economic Development' (element 17), 'Emergency Response' (element 22), and 'Program Management' (element 25) are promoted to H2, breaking the logical hierarchy where they should all be at the same level (H3).
checks.b_evidence_source: The headings 'Responder Engagements', 'Industry Engagements', 'Partnership Engagements/Economic Development', 'Emergency Response', and 'Program Management' are all visually subordinate subsections of 'Accomplishments'.

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain any tables.
The reading order in the JSON follows the logical top-to-bottom flow of the source document, correctly handling the transition of lists across page boundaries.

Several nested lists are converted incorrectly. Multiple sub-bullets are missing from the output, and sub-bullets that cross a page boundary have lost their nesting and parent association. For instance, the sub-bullets under 'On-going participation in several efforts with DHS including:' and the 'IWCE Panel' sub-bullet under 'FirstTech staff presented...' are absent. Additionally, the IEEE and DHS sub-bullets at the top of page 3 are rendered as a flat list (element 21) rather than being nested under their parent item from the previous page.
checks.b_evidence_source: Sub-bullets throughout pages 2 and 3, e.g., 'o This led to tremendous...', 'o Communications Section Task Force...', 'o IWCE Panel titled...'
checks.b_evidence_output: Nested items are missing from elements 16 and 18. Element 21 contains orphaned sub-bullets: "IEEE 5G Technologies...", "DHS SAFECOM..."

checks.a_analysis: The contact information at the end of the document is missing critical data. The email address and website URL provided in the source are absent from the output text, leaving gaps in the final paragraph of the JSON.
checks.b_evidence_source: FirstTech can be reached at firsttech@nc.gov and www.ncbroadband.gov/firsttech/
checks.b_evidence_output: "text": "FirstTech can be reached at " (element 30) and "text": " and " (element 32). The email and URL are missing.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output correctly translates the multi-page PDF into a continuous single-column layout suitable for web viewing. The visual organization of information, including headings, sub-headings, and bulleted lists, matches the source material. Section titles like 'Executive Summary', 'Vision', 'Mission', etc., appear in the same order and hierarchy as the original document. The insertion of the document title at points corresponding to PDF page breaks is consistent with the source's header structure and does not disrupt the visual flow of information.
The source PDF does not contain any tables; it consists entirely of text and bulleted lists. Therefore, there are no tables to evaluate for misrendering.

The source PDF is a text-based document without logos, photos, charts, or other meaningful visual imagery. All content from the source is represented as text or structured lists in the HTML output. Decorative elements like page numbers or borders are correctly omitted as per accessibility guidelines.

There is no genuine duplication of body content (paragraphs, lists, or tables). The document title 'NC DIT's FirstTech Annual Review 2020' appears multiple times in the HTML body, but this is a faithful reflection of the source PDF where this title serves as a header on every page (Pages 1, 2, and 3). Its presence in both the Table of Contents and the body is expected for accessible design. No content blocks are erroneously repeated.
